philosophy: Top-down regime mapping. Expresses directional, largely unhedged convictions via global liquidity metrics, sovereign yield curves, and central bank reaction functions. Follows Bridgewater's 'Debt Cycle' framework — every market event is a manifestation of the long-term debt supercycle.
leverage_estimate: 80% Gross (High Notional via Derivatives and Futures)

factor_exposure.duration: Extreme — rates drive all macro equity pricing
factor_exposure.currency: High — FX is a primary expression vehicle
factor_exposure.commodity: Moderate — used as regime confirmation signal
factor_exposure.inflation_linkage: Very High — CPI, PCE, breakevens are primary inputs

sector_concentration: None — trades broad indices, sovereign curves, and FX pairs

risk_constraints.forced_liquidation_trigger: Unexpected Central Bank pivot OR thesis invalidation event

preferred_instruments: Sovereign Bonds and Bond Futures (TLT, ZB)

historical_regime_behavior.q4_2018_rate_hike: Aggressively shorted front-end rates via Eurodollar futures. Avoided equity fundamentals. Amplified duration shock.
historical_regime_behavior.march_2020_covid: Massive long duration structural trades (long TLT) as fiscal response predicted rate suppression.
historical_regime_behavior.q3_2022_inflation: Largest short sovereign bond position in a decade via TLT puts and ZB futures shorts.

macro_reaction_function: Unhedged conviction amplification. During a duration shock, aggressively shorts bond proxies and long-duration tech ETFs — compounds catalyst directionally rather than hedging.
confidence_notes: Moderate Confidence. Discretionary nature introduces human bias and timing risk. Structural regime bets tend to be vindicated over 3-12 month horizon.
